Description.Name: The Hunt Begins
Description.Details: The Argo's command deck is crowded but focused. Marcus Hammerick and Dr. Tilsaye appear on split viewscreens, both looking like they haven't slept since the Argondale extraction. Darius speaks up first. "Marcus, Dr. Tilsaye, we need to brief you on what happened. Joanna contacted us directly. She knows we have the archive and she knows Dr. Tilsaye is no longer in her custody." Darius leans forward. "During the confrontation, she let something slip. She mentioned Fröislandis VII. We believe that's where the weapon is located." Marcus's expression shifts immediately. "She gave us a location? That's the intelligence we've been missing. Fröislandis VII, even deeper into the Periphery then. That has to be where she's completing final assembly." Dr. Tilsaye then speaks up, her expression grave. "Before we discuss tactical plans, you need to understand what the Armageddon project actually is. The project designation is misleading. Armageddon is a complete weapons platform, a mech." "An enormous superheavy mech, far larger than any BattleMech successfully constructed before in human history. Previous superheavy designs were limited to approximately two hundred tons due to structural and materials constraints. This prototype exceeds that limit by a significant margin. The breakthrough came from recent advances in exotic materials research. Lattice-core structural supports, rapid-form reinforcement systems, all the research Joanna forced me to complete. This is why she needed me specifically." She pulls up technical schematics on her display. "There is a single prototype. Built at a previously unknown facility, but I would suspect we know now the location thanks to Joanna's slip. I have seen Fröislandis VII mentioned before in classified reports, but never much detail on what was going on there. Now we probably know." "The prototype also incorporates Project Rotating Fury, a parallel weapons development program I was never cleared to access. I don't know what that technology does, but the security classification was higher than anything else in the Armageddon archive. Be extremely careful, Commander." Yang breaks the silence, his voice sharp with disbelief. "A mech that large? Past the two hundred ton threshold? The structural stress alone would be catastrophic. Heat management would be impossible. You're telling us Joanna built something that shouldn't be able to function?" Tilsaye nods slowly. "The exotic materials solve problems that made superheavy combat units of this scale impossible before. Whether it functions as designed under combat conditions, I cannot guarantee. But Joanna believes it works, she's staking everything on that belief." Marcus takes over, his tone urgent and direct. "Intelligence points to Joanna activating the Armageddon weapon ahead of her original schedule. With Dr. Tilsaye extracted and the archive gone, she's forcing her remaining researchers to complete final assembly at an accelerated pace. Our sources inside the Remembrance Cell report project completion is possible within days rather than weeks, so the prototype will be ready for combat." "We've seen signs of her pulling every Remembrance Cell warrior from across the Deep Periphery back. Every fanatic and zealot who believes in her vision of historical revenge are gathering. The facility on Fröislandis VII is as such likely to be heavily defended." "We need to get to Fröislandis VII as fast as we can, to stop Joanna before she can deploy that weapon against a major population center to enact her genocidal plans. Or worse, before she proves the concept works and starts building more of them. This prototype cannot be allowed to survive." He transmits environmental data. "Fröislandis VII is a small frozen planet at the edge of the Fröislandis system. The world is covered in polar ice caps and tundra biomes. Extreme cold, low visibility conditions, atmospheric interference affecting sensor performance. There's a known small research outpost on the planet, but it's likely far from the facility location. The harsh environment will degrade mech performance. Heat dissipation will be excellent, but mobility and targeting will suffer."He pauses, visibly troubled. "I've rallied what loyal Minnesota Tribe warriors I could find who are willing to stand against her. It's a small force, nothing like the numbers she commands, but it will have to be enough. Even I will be there." "I hope you will join us. You have already proved your combat capabilities and with your strength at your side we might even stand a chance to survive. Please accept this final contract, Commander. Let me know your decision as soon as you have one." The transmission ends. The crew looks to you for the decision.

Description.Name: There is no real choice. Accept the contract and prepare for travel to Frölandis VII.
There is no real choice. Accept the contract and prepare for travel to Frölandis VII.
Description.Details: Darius runs a quick readiness assessment, pulling up status reports on his console. "Boss, we're battered from the Argondale engagement. Mechs are damaged, armor needs replacement, ammunition reserves are lower than I'd like. Crew is exhausted." He looks up, something resolute in his expression. "But morale is the highest it's been since we started this campaign. Everyone knows what we're fighting for now. We stop a weapon designed to exterminate civilian populations, that gives people a purpose." Yang checks weapons inventory. "We can fight. We just need to get there and see what we're up against." Sumire pulls up navigation data. "Fröislandis VII coordinates are locked. We need to move immediately. Every day we delay gives Joanna more time to prepare her defenses or build more of those monstrosities." Farah's voice is quiet but resolute. "This is about preventing genocide. We all know what that weapon is designed to do. We stop it or millions die." Darius speaks last. "We go, one last trip before we can finish this whole sordid story and go back to our normal lives." Yang is already moving toward the mech bay. "Finally. Let's end this." [A new flashpoint has been created in the Fröislandis system to continue the campaign]
